audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra offering instruction text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for guilt/reparation offering, priestly portions, peace/well-being offering types, thanksgiving, vow/freewill, fat/blood prohibitions, wave/contribution portions.

verses.restored: This is instruction/Torah of guilt/reparation offering: Holy of Holies it is.
In place where burnt offering is slaughtered, guilt/reparation offering shall be slaughtered, and its blood/life shall be thrown against altar all around.
All its fat he shall bring near from it: fat tail, fat covering inward parts,
two kidneys and fat on them by loins, and lobe on liver; with kidneys he shall remove it.
The priest shall turn them into smoke on altar, fire-offering to YHWH; it is guilt/reparation offering.
Every male among priests may eat it; in holy place it shall be eaten. Holy of Holies it is.
Like sin/misalignment offering, so guilt/reparation offering; one Torah/instruction for them. The priest who makes atonement/covering with it, to him it shall belong.
The priest bringing near burnt offering of a man, skin of burnt offering that he brought near belongs to priest.
Every grain offering baked in oven, and all made in pan and on griddle, belongs to priest who brings it near.
Every grain offering mixed with oil or dry belongs to all sons of Aaron, each as his brother.
This is instruction/Torah of sacrifice of peace/well-being offering that one brings near to YHWH.

If he brings it near for thanksgiving, then he shall bring near with sacrifice of thanksgiving unleavened cakes mixed with oil, unleavened wafers spread with oil, and cakes of fine flour well mixed with oil.
With cakes of leavened bread he shall bring near his offering with sacrifice of thanksgiving of his peace/well-being offerings.
From it he shall bring near one from each offering as contribution to YHWH; it shall belong to priest who throws blood/life of peace/well-being offerings.
Flesh of sacrifice of thanksgiving peace/well-being offering shall be eaten on day of bringing-near; none of it shall be left until morning.
If sacrifice of his offering is vow or freewill offering, on day he brings near his sacrifice it shall be eaten, and on next day remainder from it may be eaten.
What remains from flesh of sacrifice on third day shall be burned with fire.

If any flesh of sacrifice of his peace/well-being offering is eaten on third day, it shall not be accepted; it shall not be credited to one bringing it near. It shall be offensive, and soul/life eating from it shall bear iniquity.
Flesh touching any unclean thing shall not be eaten; with fire it shall be burned. As for flesh, everyone clean may eat flesh.
But soul/life who eats flesh from sacrifice of peace/well-being offering that belongs to YHWH while uncleanness is upon him, that soul/life shall be cut off from its people.

When a soul/life touches any unclean thing, human uncleanness, unclean beast, or any unclean detestable thing, and eats from flesh of sacrifice of peace/well-being offering that belongs to YHWH, that soul/life shall be cut off from its people.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, saying: Any fat of ox, sheep, or goat you shall not eat.
Fat of carcass and fat of torn animal may be used for any work, but eating, you shall not eat it.
For everyone eating fat from beast from which fire-offering is brought near to YHWH, the soul/life eating shall be cut off from its people.
Any blood/life you shall not eat in any of your dwellings, from bird or beast.
Any soul/life who eats any blood/life, that soul/life shall be cut off from its people.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, saying: One bringing near sacrifice of peace/well-being offering to YHWH shall bring his offering to YHWH from sacrifice of his peace/well-being.
His hands shall bring fire-offerings of YHWH: fat with breast he shall bring, the breast to wave it as wave offering before YHWH.
The priest shall turn fat into smoke on altar, and breast shall belong to Aaron and his sons.
Right thigh you shall give as contribution to priest from sacrifices of your peace/well-being offerings.
The one among sons of Aaron who brings near blood/life of peace/well-being offerings and fat, right thigh shall be his as portion.

For breast of wave offering and thigh of contribution I have taken from children of Israel, from sacrifices of their peace/well-being offerings, and I have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons as age-long due from children of Israel.
This is anointed portion of Aaron and anointed portion of his sons from fire-offerings of YHWH, on day he brought them near to serve as priests to YHWH,
which YHWH commanded to give them on day he anointed them from children of Israel, age-long statute throughout generations.
This is Torah/instruction for burnt offering, grain offering, sin/misalignment offering, guilt/reparation offering, filling/ordination offering, and sacrifice of peace/well-being,
which YHWH commanded Moses on Mount Sinai, on day he commanded children of Israel to bring near their offerings to YHWH, in wilderness of Sinai.

translation_notes: Peace/well-being offering includes thanksgiving, vow, and freewill contexts; timing rules preserve holy meal integrity.
Leavened bread appears with thanksgiving peace offering, proving leaven is not universally negative.
Fat/blood prohibitions remain ritual reverence for abundance/life, not body-shame.
Priestly portions are structured provision from offerings, not private profiteering.
Chapter closes as summary Torah for main offering categories.
safety_note: Do not use cut-off language for modern shaming or exclusion. Preserve ritual seriousness while reading within ancient covenant meal/holiness boundaries.
